description:
Evaluate the image seeing of the Messier 12 V-band image. 

1. Open AstroImageJ and load the FITS image located at `~/AstroImages/raw/Vcomb.fits`.
2. Open the Aperture Settings and configure the aperture photometry tool with the following exact radii:
   - Radius of object aperture: 10
   - Inner radius of background annulus: 15
   - Outer radius of background annulus: 25
3. Using the single-step aperture photometry tool (Left-Click or Alt-Left-Click), select and measure exactly 5 bright, isolated, unsaturated stars in the image.
4. Save the resulting Measurements table as a CSV or tab-separated file to `~/AstroImages/measurements/seeing_data.csv`.
5. Calculate the average of the FWHM column (often labeled `FWHM_Mean`, `FWHM_Pixels`, or `Width`) for your 5 measured stars.
6. Create a text file at `~/AstroImages/measurements/seeing_report.txt` containing a single line exactly formatted as: `Average FWHM: <value>` (replace <value> with the calculated average to 2 decimal places).
7. Leave the image open with the aperture overlays visible on the stars.
